title: Getting Around
blurb: Trains, taxis, walking. Most Japanese transit signs you'll need to read are romanized on this app.

phrases.use: The universal location question. Drop in any place name. Tap the slot to type yours.
Show the address on your phone. Best taxi phrase — driver just nods.
Tell a taxi driver where. Or an info-center staffer to confirm a route.
Taxi fare estimate before you get in. Drivers won't quote too aggressively.
Trains are the default way to travel between cities. Stations are everywhere.

phrases.romaji: ~ yuki wa dono hoomu desu ka?
phrases.use: Standing in a station, lots of platforms. Ask staff at the info booth.
Before boarding a train in an unfamiliar station. Spot-check by showing the destination in romaji on your phone.
At JR stations with numbered platforms.
phrases.note: Ban-sen = track line. Nahn-BAHN-sen des ka.

phrases.romaji: shinkansen no shitei-seki o onegai shimasu
phrases.en: A reserved seat on the shinkansen, please
phrases.use: At any JR ticket office. Specify shinkansen + reserved = they book. Buy early in peak season.
phrases.note: shinkansen no shee-TAY-seh-kee oh neh-GAI she-MAS.

phrases.use: Cheaper than reserved, available same-day, but you might not get a seat.

phrases.romaji: IC kaado ni chaaji shitai desu
phrases.en: I want to top up my IC card
phrases.use: Suica/Pasmo/ICOCA cards. Tap your card at any ticket machine or ask station staff.
phrases.note: IC = integrated circuit, the standard English nickname for stored-value transit cards.

phrases.use: Most ticket machines, vending machines, and chain restaurants take IC cards.
phrases.note: Say 'IC' if they're not familiar with the specific brand name.

phrases.en: Do you have a day pass?
phrases.use: Unlimited rides for a day. Subway/bus pass types vary by city; ask the staff which suits you.
Useful when getting off at the wrong station or checking if a taxi is worth it.
Quick check before committing to walking.
When someone is pointing you down a street, this confirms 'just keep going' vs turn.

phrases.romaji: migi desu ka, hidari desu ka?
phrases.use: Confirm before turning at a fork.

phrases.en: Show me on the map, please
phrases.use: Big-brain fallback for any direction problem. Front-desk staff have maps; so do most taxi drivers on their phones.
At your hotel, restaurant, or shop front. They dial for you; the taxi arrives in 5-10 min usually.

phrases.en: What time is the last train?
phrases.use: Critical at night — 'last train' (shuuden / saishuu ressha) is the city's bedtime.
phrases.note: SHOO-den wah NAHN-jee des ka. 'shuuden' = last train.

phrases.use: Big stations (Tokyo, Shinjuku, Shibuya, Shinagawa) have multiple lines. Ask before boarding.
To a station staff after realizing you boarded the wrong way. They will direct you to the opposite platform.
phrases.note: Hahn-tai HOH-koh desh-TA. Lit. 'it was the opposite direction'.
